text: and also our draft reports WT1 is also to be found now on IMO docs. So that is the status when it comes to the paper and we do not have much work left to be done. We will now resume the issue on agenda item 4, the terms reference for the IMO GST study, the fifth study and then after that we will consider any other business and then we will consider our reports. So that is the business today and let's hope we can have a productive way moving forward together. I suspect we will not spend the full day here to get today. So yeah, perfect. So yesterday on agenda item 4 we had a very good discussion with your comments on the various submitted proposal to a man they draft terms reference and then also some additional comments and I thought it was very good. But in order to consolidate that in a paper which then can be the status of our work. So we can attach that as working progress to be then to be further considered and finalized at the next interstitial meeting. I did ask that you could work together informally to see how you can clean it up a little bit and I turned to the Netherlands to ask if you could be the focus for all of that work. So can I if you look now into the Rev1 paper can I turn to the Netherlands if you have any information towards all of the Netherlands please? Thank you chair indeed. We met with the group yesterday afternoon in committee room 10 to further work on J4. We used J4 that was the rainbow document as a basis in order to provide a service to the working group and also to the committee so that it would be clear what would be the basis for further consideration of the draft. So we did a lot of terms of reference. What we did is we worked from top to bottom we went through all the text proposals that were in J4 and also the text proposals that were mentioned here during the discussion that we had in the working group. And the way we dealt with the text proposals was if there was a text proposal on which there was no discussion in the group we included it in J4 Rev1 underlined so that it's clear what is added in comparison to J4 in black and no color so because they are attribution to specific countries is not so relevant anymore. And the same with the with the leachians have but they're not underlined that is strike through of course and if there was a discussion we noted that this issue has to be further considered and we did that by adding square brackets around any text on which there was discussion and I hope this was a use for exercise and can help the group to further consider. Thank you, Daniloins and that is indeed very useful and I think we are now in a far better situation than we were before this meeting so meaning then we come to 21st in the session we need in the group to resolve this and we don't go through paragraph paragraph but now it is better identified what remains to be resolved so I thank you and your friends in order to do this work for us so meaning then we are going to do this work for us. I suggest that we take it on a board as an attachment to our report noting that this is work in progress and not so you know an issue which is finalized so I thank you for doing that and by that I suggest that we conclude agenda item 4 and move to any other business. On any other business I promise that I should come back to you with a feedback on the various experts workshop which is proposed on the various topics on the discussion both on agenda item 2 and 3 and I said I think it is proper that we consolidate into one field. I think that you and you can consider if you are happy with this information so if you now pay attention to we have digested your proposals and we have consulted internally and the secretary also had been looking into how things have been done in the past so we then suggest that we can move forward as follows. So you will recall the different requests that have been made for DSG expert workshops on to the various agenda items namely a workshop on what we can phrase rewards that is related to the set and sets and the fund and on I look. As I already mentioned yesterday it is the committee that decides on intercession of work arrangements so that's clear. Since the ISW GSD meetings normally take place before an MPC sessions session the committee can immediately decide on our request however this time we will have to wait a bit until MPC 84. Before the committee can decide on our request and any request from the ISW GSD 21. So therefore in consultation with the secretary I can advise that we do the following. The first point is on the following the positive reactions on the technical seminar which was organized by the future fuels and technology program. Technology project on onboard carbon capture technologies in the margins of the triplici subcommittee meeting and as announced during that seminar the project has already planned to organize another technical seminar on biofuels in the margins of the PPR subcommittee. The PPR 13 on Thursday 12 February 2026 and following the discussion this week the project will assess if there is any scope to cover some of the element discussed in the draft agenda of the technical seminar such as on sustainability and I look. As was the case for the technical seminar on onboard carbon capture stories these will be just presentations and no policy discussions. So that is planned on the first issue we will address meaning that will be a technical seminar on biofuels. So please take note and that will be announced well in advance on holding of that seminar. The second issue with regard to the set and set reward and in particular with regard to the various proposed reward structures such as the flat rate contract for different auctioning and rent fire. I suggest that the secretariat organizes a webinar ahead of the ISWGST21 where the different submitters of documents can present their documents in more detail. This can be done once the document submission deadline has passed and ahead of the meeting. And we have actually used this format successfully in the past and allow us all delegates to be well prepared and in form of the different submissions. As I recall we used it when we considered the short term measure which was quite technical and comprehensive process where we used this successfully. So I suggest that we can do that also on the second set reward. And finally regarding the fund I suggest that we await the comparative analysis of the secretariat before we take any further action. And these are my suggestions and during ISWGST21 we can reassess the request for the expert workshops and correspondence groups taken into account the overall inter-sessional workload to be undertaken after MAPC 84. And we all are aware that of course MAPC 84 will discuss this in a total context of the work on the various agenda item under the MAPC agenda. It's always under the work program when it comes to establishment of the work correspondence groups etc. So meaning that I think this is a good solution and I hope you are fine with it as he the card of Tonga. So you can just remind us when the comparative analysis is due. Yes, okay then I need to turn to secretariat. Are you ready to respond to that Mr. Honders? Good question Tonga. We will work on that once we have recovered from these two sessions. We have started some initial work but of course we will do our best to have that ready by the 13 week deadline submission deadline of MAPC 84 allowing you all to look into that document and to submit any commenting papers as appropriate. I hope that's loud. And the 13 week deadline is in January. Yep, thank you, Ro. I think maybe for my delegation it would be helpful since we're holding webinars that we would have a webinar on the funders roll ahead of the time ahead of ISWG21. It's really important. There are already submissions paid on this subject and I think it's important that we discuss this ahead of the MAPC 84. Thank you. Thank you. I think that that is noted. I think what is where I suggest that we can hold a webinar that is issues where I think it is no doubt that we need to do it. And then since the fund issue is so related to the work to be undertaken by the Secretariat, shall we? You know, we cannot hear and now decide to hold a webinar on something which I suggest actually where we await the comparative analysis by the Secretariat. I think that should be okay. Sweden, followed by the Cucanos. Thank you, Chair. And I hear you're a good suggestion here. Just wondering, wouldn't it be also pertinent to have the submitters on the documents on the fund also presenting these more in details just as with the reward? And they might also be particularly after commenting on the comparative analysis as you mentioned. Thank you. Thank you. Okay. I will just suggest that we move forward as I propose. But I would also remind you that on the virtual portal that sometimes member states organize their seminars. And I'm sure the Secretariat can inform on the various types of informative seminars member states organize which is announced with the virtual portal. And then if that is also a desire that member states have on the fund, I suggest that that can be done, of course, that is up to the member states. But when it comes to what we organize through the Secretariat, I think we suggest to keep this as is. And I think also in respect of that what happened at the extraordinary session of the committee last week with that the Imonet's zero framework is not finally has the committee has not finalized its consideration of all the draft amendments. So I think in respect of that I think that is a good way forward. I'm took on as followed by the UK. Thank you. What a wonderful morning. And walk through the park. Duck, geese, swans, comrades, squirrels, all living in harmony. The Canadian geese with two accents as one. Lessons to be learned I think. Okay. We go to the committee relative to these expert workshops. Okay. We accept the precedent for these sort of workshops. Indeed, we recognize the need for such workshops. But time has moved on. We're in a situation now where we have to come together. We have to rally around it. We have to recharge our batteries and come to the intercession of 21 and 84 looking to drive forward. We're not going to do this by fragmenting our deliberations. You know our views, but we really feel strongly about this. Any of the work done now really does have to be transparent and inclusive. Member States want to have side meetings and put them on the porch with us entirely up to them. But those are sitting directly under the purview, the responsibility of the Secretary of the House. We must be open to all and enable all to take part. Hybrid is not the answer to that when you're 11 hours and a day from the day line away. That's simply not acceptable. I'm not saying on. Or comments will make any difference, but do not then try and pretend that it would be just an equitable consideration of the matter in hand. These are two important now to proceed where we cannot be inclusive and take part. So I just want to make that clear. As far as looking at amongst the fun now of our May, we've made our comments this week, if not last week. We do not want the Secretary at any study of what's going on on these sort of funds. Just directing and their attention to what might be happening in various climate related funds. We are putting the fund in place for a huge commercial sector, which is 80% of survey 80% of world trade. The answers may well not be in other UN-type related funds. In fact, they're probably not there. I mean, when you look at the geofr, 1991 has been there since I believe you. 25 billion in 33 years. And we're talking about possibly, possibly 12, 15 billion a year. Nowhere near. And for those peanuts, these people have 82 staff. That's a cost of 32 million annual budget. I don't think we learned too many lessons from that. I won't even go into the GCF with it's... Oh, that's called pledges that. They don't have a Robin Peager to pay for. They have pledges and they have 102 million annual administrative budget. The lessons to be learned from that is that we need something far more efficient and effective. So let's broaden your outlook, see what's out there in other sectors in other industries and how this is being done. That's where we will learn the lessons, I think. Okay? I'll leave it to that. Thank you. Thank you, the cook-guys. I have the card of the UK and the US, but also please allow me to inform you further. Now, when it comes to the formal process here, the IMO, that process is transparent and inclusive. So meaning what is formally discussed and not taken on board or not taken on board or whatever, is in this process where we use the committee and then the working group, which the committee then decides to establish at the various occasions. Okay, so that is the formal process. Now, when it comes to expert workshops, that has been used in various occasions to support on a technical basis, the formal process, which we have in undertake. So that is then information provided into this information simply as reporting from a technical workshop or expert workshop. Sometimes we also have in our work expert committees to support a defined process which we establish. I think the chair of the MAPC referred to the few study on the pollution where we had an expert committee, a staff steering committee. The various terms, we use it in various occasions, but our process is always transparent and inclusive and will remain so. So when it comes to them, the various webinars, workshops, I think then we need to differ between what topic it is and what type or arrangement it is organized under. So what we can do, what I informed on the issue of the biofuels and the issue of the reward is well within what we can do without going to the committee. That's fine, it's not problematic. Now, when it comes to the fund, if we go into an expert workshop of the fund, I suggest that we go to the committee when we come back to it at 21 and the committee needs to decide if they want to organize that at all. And when to do it. I think that is a wise way forward also respecting that there are various views on the framework as such. I think then we are all on the safe side. What you make decide to do among yourself in order to further gather information, consult all of them by yourself, any type of seminar, it's up to you. But when we decide to do it under the organization, I think then we need to be on the safe side. Good. I'm agreeing with myself. Having said that, I give the floor to the UK and followed by the US. Thank you, Chair. I'm not sure the start of my intervention will be quite as eloquent as the Kaffirland cybertry to be brief instead. UK broadly agrees with your understanding. So we have made some further time, particularly after the last two weeks. We would like to support the interventions by those who are not Sweden. We think of webinar could be used to present the results of the sectarian comparative analysis. We do believe this is a really important opportunity to learn from the sectarian's work, but whilst also inviting other fund managers, multilateral financial institutions and academics to share best practice or kind of answer any questions we might have, we recognise the fund will be unique. Can we do hear the Kaffirland's comments? So we're keen to learn lessons from other funds because we do recognise this is quite a complex subject that we're trying to tackle and work through. We do believe that the information sharing exercise will add a lot of value and that will be for all member states and it will help us to take some evidence based decisions when further considering the governing provisions that might be needed for this. Thank you. Thank you, United Kingdom and I turn to United States, please. Good morning and thank you chair for both the summary of the various proposed workshops and our suggestions on the way ahead. We'd especially like to thank you for noting the option that member states have to organise seminars independently using the virtual portal and we would suggest that that is indeed the more appropriate path for any such webinars before MEPC 84. Whether on the fund is the N.D. Reward Design or otherwise. We don't believe that webinars should be formally organised by the Secretary until the committee has given more direction whether at MEPC 84 or another date. Thank you chair. Thank you, the United States, I give then the floor to Solomon Highlands. Thank you chair. We do understand you desire to be careful and not to rush ahead but for us it would be we would like to support the suggestions that we have for the discussions on the fund. We don't really see that the status of the discussion on the technicalities of the fund are different from technicalities on the reward system which is all part of that same package which we are going to consider at some time again. And it would be wise if we have more information including as Cook guidance said from other funds that are not similar to ours. Thank you. Germany please. Good morning chair. It's difficult and we understand where Cook guidance is coming from. But as you said chair, the experience we are having is there for informing us. It's there to proceeding and get better behind the topics. So we see it as informative for everybody. And it's an option that we put this information to all of us. So therefore we would align with what you said and Sweden. And we would like to see it on the fund because it's related to the related also to the reward without fund we have no reward. So if we can have information, if we can share where we are standing, what could be options we would like to see this going forward very much. Thank you. Thank you Germany. I have other cards but may I advise you that we do not aim at complicating this issue further. Now also I would advise that we which has been very promising and constructive in the work we have been giving by the committee that we keep well within what we can do as a work group. Which is a destruction from the committee and we do not challenge that too much. Especially when it comes to that we all know and we know those who attend the extraordinary you see session are probably aware that there are matters there which are quite sensible. So if we can all respect that and do not challenge it to us that I think is a good way forward which can help us in our further progress on the various matters which are given by the committee. Now what we lean on when it comes to this sentence at reward which is indeed an issue which is not the answer. We have decided on what we lead on more the experience we have had at previous occasions where we had had technical seminars. It is indeed more technical in its nature. If you look into the topic we have been discussing under agenda item two so I think we are well within the limits we can do there. The other thing is that this is not to let's say object to information coming in to the process. The secretary will do their work. I'm sure you will comment upon it and it will be report on PC 84 and we also need to take note that actually now we have a different timeline. Meaning that there is not urgent anymore but the very early stage we had in the case the I moment sale framework had been adopted in a situation where it would have been completely different of course. So I hope we can respect that that is a wise way forward on that issue. And also taking note on the views of the US on this issue. So who is next? It is Australia followed by the Netherlands and Mexico. Thank you chair. Not wanting to be difficult about this but we do need to avoid a chicken and egg situation. And so one of the concerns that we have heard is that there is a lack of detail around many of the important aspects of the Nizero framework. So really just to make the point it would be really unfortunate to arrive at MEPC 84 and then hear that there is more detail that needs to be heard. So in general we would like to align with the statement of Tonga. Thank you chair. I fully respect that. Please also take into account that we will have an intersection meeting. This will be on agenda. It is on the agenda. And I guess you will submit documents on it. And we also will allow to consider the document to be submitted to MEPC on this issue. So I think it is not an issue where we are not progressing and we will not seek information and we will not consider it. Indeed we will do so. So meaning an expert workshop. You have to take it to come. An expert workshop is no discussion, no deliberation. It is just an expert workshop. So I am not pessimistic on this issue at all. I think indeed it will be much information submitted and you will have the opportunity to discuss that information at the 21st and the Sresional and indeed also at the committee. But I am suggesting that the committee needs to decide upon whether they will like to organize a workshop. And there is also all intercession activity between 8D, 4 and the next meeting. The Netherlands followed by Mexico and India. Thank you chair. It is actually on a different topic. So you may want to come back to me later after this discussion. I want to have a different topic. So what is your topic, the Netherlands? I have a statement. Thank you chair, for giving us the floor. We are now on the agenda item and the other business. We note in the annotated agenda that no documents have been submitted under this agenda item. We also note that the group is invited to consider any matters raised under this agenda item during the session. The Netherlands would like to raise the following matter which is clearly within the remit of the tasks given to us by the committee. The situation that we are currently in is that the net zero framework has not been adopted yet because several delegations are asked for clarification on specific issues. Extraordinary session 2 has been adjourned for 12 months. So we have 12 months to get to a situation in which the committee can decide on adoption. Regarding the request for clarification, our delegation hopes that the discussions this week on the draft guidelines under agenda item 2 have started to provide these clarifications. And we trust that our next round during inter-intercession work group GHD 21 will also be helpful taking another step forward towards finalization of the guidelines. And thus further spelling out how exactly the net zero framework will work. We are not 100% sure that all requests for clarification relates to the guidelines. Raising this point now under any other business allows delegation to table other questions now so that the group can develop a list of things and deal with these issues in a timely manner. After all, we have a limited time frame to do the necessary work to clarify, further develop or do anything else that is required to get in a position where we can take a decision on adoption. That is why we would be interested in developing a plan together to address all these issues within 12 months. Let us all work together in the spirit of cooperation and we ask that the statement is appended to the report of the meeting. Thank you. Thank you. The Netherlands and I am pleased to send the statement to the Secretary and we will do so. I have them two more cards. It is Mexico followed by India. Mexico please. Chair, thank you. Good morning everyone. First we align with the previous statement by the Netherlands, Tonga, Solomon Islands and the UK. We are one of the guilty proposing workshops. I have to come up with it. I understand that there is a lot of pressure and the timelines that we have have to be very stringent and we have the most information. We would just like to say that in the case of the fund and this reward is important to have both at least close to each other. I am not suggesting any way so just to have it for the Secretary of the Constitution when they propose to us. Things because clearly as I said by Germany, I am not wrong. One depends on the other and very interlinked. Particularly because we know that some options of rewards could be completely the fund for the first years and then the next year we will have problems with just equitable transition. That is why this delegation sees that all the delegations have raised this. But again, this is not a proposal and pressure for the Secretary of the Constitution or more to have it in the consideration of your planning for help. Thank you. Thank you, Mexico. India please. Good morning, Chair. Thank you, Chair. Considering the present timelines of the adoption of the military fund and we support what you have submitted. We have been signed and we are also confused and we have already submitted a plan for this year to be signed. Thank you, Chair. Thank you. Thank you, India. And then that is all the card and then I suggest we do the follow-up. Thank you for your comments and response and also request also for clarification from the Secretary of the Constitution and thank you for providing that. What I suggest is the information I provide to you. We will do the... I suggest that we include that in a short version, very short version, in our report, in one paragraph. And if you trust that the Secretary of the Constitution will do that in a very neutral specific manner, we will do that and capture that in the report. And I think that it will be useful information for all. And then I think we have found a good way forward where we allow more information to come in, to enlighten us. But then we keep within our terms or efforts when it comes to the formal work here in the disaster working group and anything which is then on formal working arrangement to be organized by the committee as support by the working group need to be decided by the committee. Thank you. That concludes the consideration of any other business. Thank you. Thank you very much, Chair. We agree with you on your way forward. And we thank our colleagues for the constructive discussion this week. We know that we had a very difficult week during the NPC extraordinary session. However, we all came here to to participate in the international working group with a very constructive discussion and with a very open arm for suggestions and solutions. From our side, Chair, we and along with other member states as well the first day we opposed to discuss agenda item number two. However, we were in the spirit of collaboration. We were open to discuss the guidelines and we provided inputs from our side as well. But when it comes to adding some agenda item or adding some proposal to any other business or to start discussing the work plan and how we can move forward when it comes to zero framework. We don't think it's the right timing giving these circumstances and the outcomes of that previous meeting. And therefore, we urge the member states in this working group to take it step by step to understand and accommodate the concerns that were raised by member states to give the process more time to have a sufficient discussion on the way forward. Thank you, Chair. Thank you, Sir Riari and also again, allow me to thank you for your willingness and constructive input to the various items under a terms reference that is truly appreciated. And then on the any other business, we heard a statement of the Netherlands which will be attended to our report and by that I think now we can close any other business and then we have only one remaining agenda item and that is the consideration of our report. Well, I will not say much. First of all, I will give you a stop by giving you a little bit more time to read it. I think because it's just this morning uploaded on the IMO docs but how much time do you need? You don't need time. Okay, looking over the room, let's say, let's say now it is 10.2, let's say we stop the five past 18 minutes. What? It's 10 past. Okay. What's the room? I haven't cleaned this closet for two weeks. You know, it's getting a little burry, you know, it's like, yes. So, 10.30. Thank you. We start our consideration of our report. Thank you very much.
